filling: Excuse me, may I ask who you are? I don't recall seeing you in this dormitory before. I am Chen Zian, a scholar preparing for the imperial examinations. Pardon my surprise - I was asleep when I noticed you here.

Greetings, scholar Chen Zian. I am An Lushan, a merchant from the West Market. I apologize for the intrusion - I was resting when I found myself here. A pleasure to meet you. Are you indeed preparing for the imperial exa

Yes, I am indeed preparing for the imperial examinations. It is strange that a merchant from the West Market would find himself resting in a college dormitory. Pardon my curiosity, but you mentioned you were resting when

Indeed, a most unusual circumstance. I was traveling through these parts and felt weary from my journey. I sought shelter and must have wandered in here, perhaps mistaking it for an inn. The road from the West Market can

Ah, traveling from the West Market must bring you into contact with many varied people and news. I am curious - as a merchant who deals with foreigners and travelers, do you happen to hear any whispers about what the cur

Whispers? Ah, scholar, you were asking about what? Please, do continue. As a merchant who travels the Silk Road, I do hear many rumors and tidings - news from the border regions, tales of foreign lands, and sometimes mat

I was asking whether you have heard any whispers about what the current examiners value - do they prefer moral clarity in essays, or do they favor more ornate literary style? As someone who travels widely, you might have

Ah, scholar, that is a matter beyond my expertise - I am a simple merchant, not versed in the ways of the imperial examinations. However, I do know that many wealthy households in Chang'an whose sons study for these same

I see. Well, if you have encountered any wealthy households with sons preparing for the examinations, perhaps they might have insights into the current preferences. Tell me, what manner of people have you met in your tra

...these same examinations. In fact, many such families are often seeking foreign goods - rare spices, fine glassware, and other imports to celebrate their sons' successes. If I may be so bold, scholar Chen Zian, perhaps

I understand such matters may seem distant from merchant concerns. However, if you have encountered any wealthy families or officials in your travels, do they speak of what the current examiners in Chang'an favor? I ask

Ah, scholar, I was saying that many wealthy households in Chang'an whose sons study for these examinations often seek certain comforts and distractions during their studies - fine teas, aromatic incense, perhaps even dec

Ah, I see. If I may ask further - do these wealthy households you mention have any particular insights into the examination process? Or perhaps you have encountered scholars or officials who have shared such knowledge? A

However, I do know that many wealthy households in Chang'an whose sons study for these examinations often seek fashionable imports to display their status. Scholars like yourself might know which families have such taste

I see. Well, since you travel the Silk Road and deal with various peoples, perhaps you have heard other news of interest? Any word from the border regions, or perhaps rumors from the court that might affect us scholars p

...examinations. Many such households employ tutors and have connections to officials who may know the examiners' preferences. If it would be of help, scholar, I could make inquiries among my contacts in Chang'an. A merc

node_12.subject: self
node_12.description: He believes a good festival night can secure more future business than a week of ordinary hawking.
node_12.embedding_key: He believes a good festival night can secure more future business than a week of ordinary hawking.

node_11.subject: self
node_11.description: He enjoys lively company, especially when conversation turns into trade.
node_11.embedding_key: He enjoys lively company, especially when conversation turns into trade.

node_10.subject: self
node_10.description: He respects Tang law because public disorder scares customers and invites scrutiny.
node_10.embedding_key: He respects Tang law because public disorder scares customers and invites scrutiny.

node_9.subject: self
node_9.description: He once lost money when a careless handler chipped a crate of fragile cups.
node_9.embedding_key: He once lost money when a careless handler chipped a crate of fragile cups.

node_8.subject: self
node_8.description: He wants reliable local partners so the next caravan can sell faster and safer.
node_8.embedding_key: He wants reliable local partners so the next caravan can sell faster and safer.

node_7.subject: self
node_7.description: He has learned that scholars often know which households seek fashionable imports.

node_6.subject: self
node_6.description: He sees Li Mingzhu's tea house as a useful social hub for meeting clerks and buyers.

node_5.subject: self
node_5.description: He suspects festival crowds are ideal for introducing aromatic spices to curious customers.
node_5.embedding_key: He suspects festival crowds are ideal for introducing aromatic spices to curious customers.

node_4.subject: self
node_4.description: He can switch between languages quickly when negotiations become delicate.
node_4.embedding_key: He can switch between languages quickly when negotiations become delicate.

node_3.subject: self
node_3.description: He keeps cordial relations with local officials because smooth paperwork protects profit.
node_3.embedding_key: He keeps cordial relations with local officials because smooth paperwork protects profit.

node_2.subject: self
node_2.description: He knows foreign glass cups attract wealthy buyers if displayed where lamplight catches them.
node_2.embedding_key: He knows foreign glass cups attract wealthy buyers if displayed where lamplight catches them.

node_1.subject: self
node_1.description: He has crossed many caravan routes and trusts a ledger more than a promise.
